fatal: The ruling amends REQ-011 and AT-011 but leaves REQ-007's exact seven-field listing contract and AT-007.25 unchanged, creating a direct P0 contradiction.

prd-mvp.md defines neither a complete severity taxonomy/public label mapping nor blocker disposition on in_progress→open. The proposed highest-severity chip can therefore be undefined or reflect obsolete blockers after volunteer release.
serious: The chip is aggregate-only, so it does not directly reveal blocker type; nevertheless, state and context can make fuel, Lovable, or NGO-action blockers readily inferable.

Moving blocker state from a detail page to every marketplace card materially amplifies financial and operational exposure. That is a dignity and discoverability change even where the underlying state was already public.

A negative badge on open projects creates adverse selection precisely for abandonment/rematch projects carrying retained blockers. Goal 5 instead gives those projects rematch priority and routes unmatched aging to the concierge.

The posted date already exposes listing age. An updated-relative-time field adds an ambiguous, gameable freshness score: trivial edits can refresh it, while activity-based recency can publicly stigmatize abandoned projects. Its event source and reset rules are unspecified.

The GitHub justification is internally inconsistent. Architecture decision d38 calls the static open card load-bearing; an operational blocker count is specifically unlike GitHub repository cards, even if the showcase is intentionally richer.
A disappearing zero-state is acceptable only if absence unambiguously means zero after successful loading. Otherwise it conflates zero blockers with unavailable or stale blocker data.

A severity-colored aggregate risks becoming a public negative rating of the named NGO, contrary to the spirit of no public ratings, especially when the card does not explain whether responsibility lies with the NGO, volunteer, platform, or an external dependency.
If the chip is clickable or exposes a title/type on hover, it also violates AT-011.02's exactly-one volunteer action and expands the leak beyond the proposed payload.

leaks.what_it_leaks: An unresolved scope or task question and, by implication, that the project is waiting for NGO clarification; drill-down text can expose operational details despite the data-entry warning.
That the named NGO has not completed a requested review, turning coordination delay into a public blame signal.
That delivery depends on an unresolved third party, vendor, approval, or integration; titles or body text may identify the dependency.
A repository-access or governance problem and potentially weak permission administration.
That the NGO has not completed required workspace, invitation, billing, or connection setup.
That project fuel is below 20% or exhausted; highest severity can distinguish warning from a hard stop and thereby expose the NGO's qualitative financial state.
That the NGO's separate Lovable allowance is low or exhausted, exposing subscription or funding readiness.
An arbitrary operational problem and potentially which party is expected to act; this is the broadest accidental-disclosure channel.

open_state_validity: A newly published open project normally cannot have volunteer-raised, repo, setup, review, or external-dependency blockers because no volunteer or kickoff exists. A fuel blocker is plausible because fuel can be purchased and consumed during Discovery before matching. A matched_pending_fuel→open expiry is still pre-build and ordinarily adds no build blocker. The real nonempty case is in_progress→open abandonment/rematch: fuel, history, and apparently every unresolved blocker survive because REQ-024 archives only on completion and REQ-005.5 specifies no release cleanup. That makes the chip nonempty in a real edge case, but also exposes a lifecycle bug: volunteer-specific or setup-instance blockers may be stale or ownerless after release.
at_impact: REQ-007's listing clause and AT-007.25 must change; the candidate's AT-011-only amendment is insufficient.
AT-011.05 must define whether recency and blocker summary are data attributes and replace the exact-seven assertion if the ruling is retained.
AT-011.06 must permit only the explicitly named signals while continuing to reject task progress, cadence, stack, contributor, dollar, and candidacy data.
AT-011.08 and AT-011.14 both need blocker assertions. AT-011.14 is especially important because its setup-pending fixture should normally create a blocker.
AT-011.10 and the coverage map must stop calling the open card static if any dynamic signal remains.
AT-011.02 must prove the chip is noninteractive and does not become a second volunteer action; AT-011.07 and AT-011.09 remain unchanged hard negatives.

The future REQ-024 suite must test zero-state absence after successful loading, count, highest-severity ordering, auto-resolution, abandonment reopening, obsolete-blocker cleanup, completion archival, and absence of type/title/body leakage.
Recency needs controlled-clock tests defining its source events, behavior on unpublish/republish and abandonment, and confirmation that it never affects newest-first ordering or tie-breaking.
repair: Keep the open marketplace card unchanged: exactly the seven identity attributes plus its mark-interest control, with posted date as its only age signal and no blocker chip. Add the generic blocker chip only to the already-operational in-progress showcase. Amend REQ-024 so marketplace observability means the in-progress showcase; open projects expose blockers on their public project page and NGO dashboard but not on the matching card. Make the showcase chip noninteractive and limited to count plus a defined public severity enum such as info, warning, and blocking—never type, title, body, action owner, dollars, or hover detail. Define in_progress→open cleanup so obsolete volunteer/setup blockers archive or retarget before rendering. Update REQ-007, REQ-011, d38 architecture notes, AT-007.25, and AT-011.05/.06/.08/.10/.14 accordingly.
rationale: The ruling does not create a wholly new confidentiality breach because REQ-010 already makes the project page identical for logged-out visitors and publicly exposes clarification, fuel, and Lovable state. It does, however, convert one-click-deeper operational information into a marketplace selection signal attached to a named NGO. That amplification matters most for abandoned projects that Goal 5 wants rematched. Combined with undefined recency semantics, undefined public severity labels, missing blocker cleanup, and an overlooked exact-field contract in REQ-007, the ruling is not sound as written. Restricting the chip to the live showcase preserves operational transparency where it is useful while protecting the open matching funnel from stigma and restoring d38's static/open versus live/in-progress split.
